label: BREAKING
caption:
Brent crude just hit $119.50/barrel. The IEA called it the "biggest energy security threat in history."

A few asterisks worth adding:

In 2008, Brent hit $147. In inflation-adjusted terms, today's price is roughly $90 in 2008 dollars.

In 1979, the Iranian Revolution removed roughly 7% of global oil supply. Today's disruption is closer to 1-2%.

— The Clam Letter
